observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended and Management agreed to secure relevant records
containing adequate information and prepare the required subsidiary ledger
to validly support the above-mentioned account balances.

1.7 We also recommended and Management agreed to reconcile with the records
of the Metro Bangued Water District (MBWD) for proper accounting and
settlement of the aforementioned obligations.

3.6 We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Engineer,
the ICO, Municipal Accountant and the GSD Officer – Designate to locate
records of road networks in their offices where the necessary data,
specifications and locations of the Road Networks may be sourced to
facilitate the preparation and submission of the aforementioned required
reports and the proper recognition of Road Networks.

3.7 We also recommended and Management agreed to ensure preparation and
maintenance of subsidiary ledgers for the roads and its components and the
disclosure thereof in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2015-008.

4.3 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all related
disclosures on LDRRMF are reflected in the Notes to Financial Statements.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the disclosure requirements on LDRRMF as
provided by COA Circular No. 2014-002.

5.4 We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to provide the Reconciliation of Surplus/(Deficit) with the Net
Cash Flow from Operating Activities for all funds. Henceforth, strictly
comply with the provisions of International Public Sector Accounting
Standards (IPSAS) for financial reporting purposes.

6.5 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
with the cooperation of the Municipal Budget Officer provide reconciliation
between actual amounts on a comparable basis to the budget and actual
amounts presented in the statement of financial performance. Henceforth,
strictly comply with the provisions of International Public Sector Accounting
Standards (IPSAS) for financial reporting purposes.

7.7 We recommended and Management agreed that FSs, DVs and ORs be
submitted within prescribed period to enable timely audit of account
balances and transactions.

8.5 We recommended and Management agreed to require the concerned
personnel to cooperate in the preparation and submission of AAPSI in order
for the audit team to monitor the status of implementation of audit
recommendations and validate the actions taken, if any.

9.8 We recommended and Management agreed to adhere to the transparency
provisions as provided by the different rules and regulations cited above.

10.10 We recommended and Management agreed:
• That the Municipal Accountant see to it that all disbursement vouchers
are properly supported with complete documentation. In no case shall a
disbursement be paid without the necessary supporting documents; and
• That the lacking documents enumerated above be immediately submitted
to COA for evaluation and review.

11.6 We recommended and Management agreed to submit to the audit team the
list of beneficiaries of the fertilizers that were supposedly procured and
distributed specifying therein their names, addresses, quantity received and
their signatures for further verification.

12.6 We recommended and Management agreed to:
• Submit the APP and supporting PPMPs for CY 2023 to COA for audit
purposes, if any; and
• Always prepare APP and supporting PPMPs which serve as a basis guide
on their procurement activities pursuant to Section 7.2 of the Revised IRR
of RA No. 9184. A copy thereof be furnished to COA for audit purposes.

13.5 We recommended and Management agreed to furnish the Auditor a copy of
the purchase orders and contract documents not later than five working days
from issuance of purchase orders and perfection of contracts together with
duly accomplished supporting documents for technical review and
evaluation in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

14.8 We recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee agreed to submit the
aforementioned required documents and comply with the rules and
regulations as required by the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

15.7 We recommended and Management agreed to submit immediately the
approved list of programs and projects to be implemented and fund
utilization report by the agency from its share from Tobacco Excise Tax and
Burley and Native Tobacco Excise Tax received in CY 2022 and 2023 together
with other relevant documents for the audit team’s evaluation.

16.6 We recommended and Management agreed to:
• Submit the Quarterly Reports on the Utilization of 20% DF using the Full
Disclosure Policy (FDP) Form 7 for audit team’s evaluation; and
• Make use that a separate depository account for 20% DF immediately for
proper monitoring and control of expenditures actually charged against
the said fund.

17.3 We recommended and Management agreed to:
• Submit the LDRRMF Utilization Report and ensure that implementation
should be based on the planned program/projects identified during the
budget process; and
• Make a report on the fund status and utilization of the LDRRMF and post
it in conspicuous places of the municipality. This is to promote
transparency and to prevent suspicions of any irregularity.

18.5 We recommended and Management agreed to immediately submit the GAD
Accomplishment Report with complete supporting documents and in the
prescribed format for audit and evaluation.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the accounting entries made for the aforementioned transactions and to draw the proposed accounting entries to reflect the correct balances of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: The fund transfer was fully liquidated

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management see to it that the DRRMF unexpended/unobligated balances are duly transferred to a special trust fund account to avoid misuse of funds and to effect proper accounting entries in compliance with the COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The amount cited was fully liquidated

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Point System to:
• Institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex- Based disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation;
• Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; and
• Revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues.
observations.managementAction: On-going updating of the Community- Based Monitoring System (CBMS)
observations.reason: No reason was provided by the Management

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and Management agreed to secure relevant
records containing adequate information and prepare the required
subsidiary ledger to validly support the above-mentioned account
balances.

We have recommended and Management agreed to implement the
following measures to assure the reliability of balances of PPE
accounts:
• Physical inventory of fixed assets shall be made at least once a
year;
• Establishment of property identification and coding;
• Maintenance of the Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment to
establish the official accountable for the property;
• Preparation of inventory reports of PPE;
• Reconciliation of the inventory report with the balances reflected
in the financial statements, identification of persons accountable
for loss of property, and proper adjustment in the financial
statements, if any; and
• Maintenance of updated Property Card and Property Plant and
Equipment Ledger Cards.

We have recommended and Management agreed to direct the
Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to locate records
of road networks in their offices where the necessary data,
specifications and locations of the Road Networks may be sourced to
facilitate the preparation and submission of the aforementioned
required reports and the proper recognition of Road Networks.

We have also recommended and Management agreed to ensure
preparation and maintenance of subsidiary ledgers for the roads and
its components and the disclosure thereof in the Notes to Financial
Statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We have recommended and Management agreed to see to it that the
DRRMF unexpended/unobligated balances are duly transferred to a
special trust fund account to avoid misuse of funds and to effect
proper accounting entries in compliance with the COA Circular No.
2012-002.

We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
disclose all the necessary information pertaining to LDRRMF.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the disclosure requirements on
LDRRMF as provided by COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
provide complete and correct Reconciliation of Surplus/(Deficit) with
the Net Cash Flow from Operating Activities for all funds.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of International
Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) for financial reporting
purposes.

We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Budget Officer agreed to provide reconciliation between
actual amounts on a comparable basis to the budget and actual
amounts presented in the statement financial performance.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of International
Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) for financial reporting
purposes.

We have recommended and Management agreed that FSs, DVs and
ORs should be submitted within the prescribed period to enable
timely audit of account balances and transactions.

We have recommended and Management agreed to adhere to the
transparency provisions as provided by the different rules and
regulations cited above.

We have recommended and Management agreed:
• That the Municipal Accountant see to it that all disbursement
vouchers are properly supported with complete documentation. In no case shall a disbursement be paid without the necessary supporting documents; and
• That the lacking documents enumerated above be immediately
submitted to COA for evaluation and review.

We have recommended and Management agreed to:
• Submit the APP and supporting PPMPs for CY 2021 to COA for
audit purposes, if any; and
• Always prepare APP and supporting PPMPs which serve as a
basis guide on their procurement activities pursuant to Section 7.2
of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. A copy thereof be furnished to
COA for audit purposes.

We have recommended and Management agreed to furnish the
Auditor a copy of the purchase orders and contract documents not
later than five working days from issuance of purchase orders and
perfection of contracts together with duly accomplished supporting
documents for technical review and evaluation in compliance with
COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We have recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee agreed
to submit the aforementioned required documents. Henceforth,
comply with the rules and regulations as required by the 2016 Revised
IRR of RA 9184.

We have recommended and Management agreed that the utilization
of the 20% Development Fund should be limited on programs,
projects and activities that will contribute to the attainment of socio-
economic development and environmental management outcomes and
shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures that
will yield greater benefit to the people in accordance with DBM-DILG
JMC No. 2017-1 and on allowable COVID-19 related expenses
enumerated in the DILG-DBM JMC No. 01 dated March 27, 2020.

We have recommended and Management agreed to immediately
submit the required supporting documents for further verification of
the submitted GAD Accomplishment Report

We have recommended and Management agreed to require the GAD
Focal Point System to:
• Institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and
systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide
the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy
formulation:
• Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender
issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are
responsive to the said issues; and
• Revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for
particular gender issues.
We recommended the lacking documents enumerated above be immediately submitted to COA for evaluation and review.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management secure relevant records containing
adequate information and prepare the required subsidiary ledger to validly
support the above-mentioned account balances.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Engineer and the
Municipal Accountant to locate records of road networks in their offices
where the necessary data, specifications and locations of the Road Networks
may be sourced to facilitate the preparation and submission of the
aforementioned required reports and the proper recognition of Road
Networks and their corresponding depreciation in the accounting books.

We also recommended that Management ensure preparation and
maintenance of subsidiary ledgers for the roads and its components and the
disclosure thereof in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended the following:
 That the Municipal Accountant exert more effort to trace and identify
the variance and reconcile all the records;
 For Management to make representation with the Land Bank of the
Philippines for the reconciliation of their individual records and obtain
other related loan documents as reference in the adjusting the books to
reflect the correct balances of accounts in the Financial Statements;
 That the Municipal Accountant maintain Subsidiary Ledger for
individual loan releases regularly reconciled with the General Ledger;
and
 For the Municipal Accountant to present properly the current and non-
current portion of the Loans Payable – Domestic account in the
Financial Statements.

We recommended that management see to it that the unexpended /
unobligated LDRRMF are treated as Trust Liability at the end of the year
for easy monitoring and proper classification and presentation of account
balances.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
disclose all the necessary information pertaining to LDRRMF. Henceforth,
strictly comply with the disclosure requirements on LDRRMF as provided
by COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
provide complete and correct Reconciliation of Surplus/(Deficit) with the
Net Cash Flow from Operating Activities for all funds. Henceforth, strictly
comply with the provisions of International Public Sector Accounting
Standards (IPSAS) for financial reporting purposes.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
with the cooperation of the Municipal Budget Officer provide reconciliation
between actual amounts on a comparable basis to the budget and actual
amounts presented in the statement financial performance. Henceforth,
strictly comply with the provisions of International Public Sector
Accounting Standards (IPSAS) for financial reporting purposes.

We reiterated our recommendation in previous AARs that FSs, DVs and
ORs should be submitted within the prescribed period to enable timely
audit of account balances and transactions.

We reiterated our recommendation in previous Annual Audit Reports that
Management furnish the Auditor a copy of the purchase orders and
contract documents not later than five working days from issuance of
purchase orders and perfection of contracts together with duly
accomplished supporting documents for technical review and evaluation in
compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

That the Municipal Accountant see to it that all disbursement vouchers
are properly supported with complete documentation. In no case shall a
disbursement be paid without the necessary supporting documents; and;

That the lacking documents enumerated above be immediately
submitted to COA for evaluation and review.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
submit the lacking documents and ensure that in future transactions all
required documents are attached and reviewed before processing the
disbursement vouchers and making payment thereof. Henceforth, ensure
that rules and regulations with regard to the procurement of medicines are
observed at all times.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
 Submit the APP and supporting PPMPs for CY 2019 for audit purposes;
and
 Always prepare APP and supporting PPMPs which serve as a basis guide
on their procurement activities pursuant to Section 7.2 of the Revised
IRR of RA 9184. A copy thereof be furnished to COA for audit purposes.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee justify the noted lapses
and submit the required documents mentioned under item 1.3.5 above. Henceforth, comply with the
rules and regulations as required by the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed to justify the above-noted
lapses. Utilization of the 20% Development Fund should be limited on
programs, projects and activities that will contribute to the attainment of
socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and
shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures that will yield
greater benefit to the constituents of the Municipality in accordance with
DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
maintain a special account and a complete set of subsidiary ledgers for the
said fund.

We recommended that management justify the noted lapses and instruct the
MPDC to discuss with the LDC the preparation, adoption and updating of
the CLUP and CDP for CY 2020 onwards.

We recommended that Management immediately submit the GAD
Accomplishment Report with complete supporting documents and in the
prescribed format for audit and evaluation.

observations.recommendation:
2.6 We have recommended that Management direct the Municipal Engineer
and the Municipal Accountant to locate records of road networks in their
offices where the necessary data, specifications and locations of the Road
Networks may be sourced to facilitate the recognition of road networks and
the corresponding depreciation in the accounting books, the preparation of
subsidiary ledgers and necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial
Statements.

2.7 We have also recommended that Management ensure compliance with the
conduct of inventory and the recognition of Road Networks in the books of
accounts as well as the disclosure thereof in the Notes to Financial
Statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008.

3.8 We have recommended that management instruct the Accountant to review
the journal entries made for the above transaction and to draw the proposed accounting entry as follows to reflect the correct balances of the
accounts:
Government Equity xxxxxxxx
Due to LGUs xxxxxxxxx

4.8 We have recommended that management see to it that the unexpended /
unobligated funds from prior and current year LDRRM are treated as
Trust Liability at the end of the year and duly transferred to a special trust
fund account to avoid misuse of funds.

5.6 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide complete
and correct Reconciliation of Surplus/(Deficit) with the Net Cash Flow
from Operating Activities for all funds. Henceforth, strictly comply with
the provisions of Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS)
for financial reporting purposes.

6.8 We have recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer maintain
Registry of Appropriations, Allotment and Obligations in addition to the
existing Treasury Operation Management System to properly monitor the
budget of the agency and serve as the basis in the preparation of the
Statement of Allotment, Appropriations, Obligations and Balances and the
SCBAA.

7.5 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose all the
necessary information pertaining to LDRRMF. Henceforth, strictly
comply with the disclosure requirements on LDRRMF as provided by COA
Circular No. 2014-002.

8.7 We have recommended that the FSs, DVs and ORs should be submitted by
Management within the prescribed period to enable timely audit of account
balances and transactions.

9.8 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer ensure that all required documents are attached and reviewed
before processing the disbursement vouchers and making payment thereof.
Ensure that rules and regulations with regard to the procurement of medicines are observed at all times.

10.5 We have recommended that management submit a copy of an approved
Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for CY 2018. Henceforth, ensure that an
APP be prepared and approved before any procurement will be made.

11.7 In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended that the Bids
and Awards Committee justify the noted lapses and submit the required
documents mentioned under item 11.3.5 above. Henceforth, comply with
the rules and regulations as required by the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

12.7 We have recommended that Management adhere strictly with the
provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of the DILG and DBM on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the IRA for
programming for development projects. Consider utilizing the fund for
projects that yield lasting benefit to the constituents of the Municipality.

13.4 We have recommended that the Sanggunian Bayan pass a board resolution
authorizing the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer to open a
separate bank account for the 20% Development Fund. Also, the
Municipal Accountant maintain a special account and a complete set of
subsidiary for the said fund.

14.6 We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The
Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the
preparation, adoption and updating of the CLUP for CY 2019 onwards.

15.7 We have recommended that management comply with the provisions that
require the allocation to GAD activities of a minimum of 5% of the
Municipality’s annual total budget.

16.4 We have recommended that management submit the Annual GAD
Accomplishment Report including the required attachments for the audit
team to ascertain the activities’ compliance with gender issues and
concerns.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant sees to it that monthly accounts are submitted to the auditor’s office together with the paid vouchers/payrolls/official receipts in accordance with pertinent rules and regulations.  The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer be aware of all the requirements and accounts to be submitted to the Commission and the deadlines of submission of such accounts to avoid sanctions and delay of the audit. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor is advised to take a look into the possibility of assigning additional personnel in the Accounting Office to facilitate submission of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management furnish the Auditor copy of the purchase orders and contract documents not later than five (5) working days from issuance of purchase orders and perfection of contracts together with duly accomplished supporting documents for technical review and evaluation in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

observations.recommendation: 3.8	We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant sees to it that monthly accounts are submitted to the auditor’s office together with the paid vouchers/payrolls/official receipts in accordance with pertinent rules and regulations.  The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer be aware of all the requirements and accounts to be submitted to the Commission and the deadlines of submission of such accounts to avoid sanctions and delay of the audit. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor is advised to take a look into the possibility of assigning additional personnel in the Accounting Office to facilitate submission of accounts.

4.8	We have recommended that management prepare a checklist of documentary requirements to be included in the disbursement vouchers.  The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer ensure that all required documents were submitted and reviewed before any payment is to be made and that abide with the rules and regulations with regard to the procurement of medicines.

5.8	We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses.  The Municipal Mayor instructs the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the preparation, adoption and updating of both plans for CY 2018 onwards.

6.4	We have recommended that management furnish the Auditor copy of the purchase orders and contract documents not later than five (5) working days from issuance of purchase orders and perfection of contracts together with duly accomplished supporting documents for technical review and evaluation in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.
7.5	We have recommended that an Annual Procurement Plan be prepared to plan and schedule the procurement activities of the Municipality in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184.

8.4	We have recommended that the Municipality either retain an amount equivalent to at least ten percent (10%) of every progress payment or any of the authorized warranty security for the procurement of goods in order to ascertain that manufacturing defects, if it falls within the prescribed period of warranty, shall be borne and corrected by the supplier.

9.13	In view of the foregoing audit observations, we have recommended to Management the following:
Justify the above noted lapses;
Submit the needed documents for verification; and
Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by RA No. 9184.

11.6	We have recommended that management prepare the LDRRMFIP annually for disaster risk reduction management program presenting the lump-sum 30% allocation for Quick Response Fund and allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded.
12.4	We have recommended that management submit the necessary GAD Plan and Budget and the accomplishment report related to the utilization of GAD funds for audit purposes.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the basis in setting up the 2016 RPT/SET Receivable as well as the schedule to support the RPT/SET Receivable and the corresponding Deferred Income account balances as of December 31, 2016; record the proposed adjusting entry; and review and analyze the nature of the recorded transactions of the above-mentioned accounts and effect necessary adjustments, if any, to present fairly the financial statements.  The Municipal Treasurer should maintain/update Taxpayer’s Index Card to keep track of the yearly collection of real property tax.  The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer should also coordinate with one another to attain proper accounting of RPT as prescribed under NGAS.  The Municipal Treasurer should also coordinate and reconcile his records with that of the Municipal Assessor to determine any changes and/or updates in the real properties.
observations.managementAction: The basis of Management in setting up the RPT/SET Receivable is the assessment roll from the Assessor’s Office. Updating of the List of Taxpayers is ongoing. Tax Index Card is also available.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above noted observations, we have recommended to Management the following:

•	That the respective personnel in charge of the noted deficiencies submit the required supporting documents; and

•	Henceforth, that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer verify whether all necessary supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers before processing the claims, otherwise the claim should be returned to the respective employee/personnel for compliance of the requirements.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management revisit its policies/procedures in hiring job order workers and see to it that the same is aligned with the latest guidelines as contained under CSC-COA-DBM Joint memorandum Circular No. 1, s. 2017.  Meantime, submit the noted missing supporting documents for evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the agency officials justify why the procedural limits provided under R.A. No. 9184 were exceeded.  We also recommend that purchases beyond the threshold on shopping and small value procurements be done through public bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR.  Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in the highly exceptional cases provided for in R.A. No. 9184.  In all instances, the procuring entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price for the Government is obtained.
observations.managementAction: Management justified that disbursement vouchers were merged according to payee and paid through one check, hence payments made seem to exceed the ceiling for shopping and small value procurement.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing audit observations, we have recommended to Management the following:

•	Justify the above noted lapses;
•	Submit the needed documents for verification; and
•	Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant submit the basis in setting up the 2015 RPT/SET Receivable as well as the schedule to support the RPT/SET Receivable and the corresponding Deferred Income account balances as of December 31, 2015; record the proposed adjusting entry; and review and analyze the nature of the recorded transactions of the above-mentioned accounts and effect necessary adjustments, if any, to present fairly the financial statements.  The Municipal Treasurer should maintain/update Taxpayer’s Index Card to keep track of the yearly collection of real property tax.  The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer should also coordinate with one another to attain proper accounting of RPT as prescribed under NGAS.  The Municipal Treasurer should also coordinate and reconcile his records with that of the Municipal Assessor to determine any changes and/or updates in the real properties.
observations.managementAction: Management claimed that their basis in setting up the RPT/SET Receivable is the assessment roll from the Assessor’s Office. Management agreed to submit the schedule to support the RPT/SET Receivable and the corresponding Deferred Income account balances.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to record the proposed adjusting journal entry, submit the lacking documents and take extra care in recording transactions by adhering to prescribed accounting regulations to present fairly the financial statements.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation and Management agreed to timely submit the required reports, financial statements and disbursement vouchers. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that monthly financial statements and reports accounts are submitted to the auditor’s office together with the paid vouchers/payrolls/official receipts in accordance with the provisions of Section 100 of PD 1445 to avoid the imposition of sanctions and delay in the conduct of audit.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that the Bids and Awards Committee justify the noted lapses and submit the needed documents for verification.  Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by RA No. 9184.

We have recommended that the one (1) year defects liability period from the project’s completion date up to the final acceptance of the projects should be fully covered by the Performance Security of the contractor.  Applicable warranty security against structural defects and failures should also be imposed by the contractors.  Likewise, the Municipal Accountant must ensure that the contractors post the warranty security covering the full amount in the first year and renew the same every year thereafter, subject to reduction every year by the amount of depreciation on a straight line basis.

Further, we have recommended that the Municipal Engineer conduct ocular inspection of the infrastructure projects after the one (1) year defects liability period, and in the event that said project manifested structural defects, oblige the contractor to undertake the necessary repair works or forfeit the corresponding warranty security.
We have recommended that management justify the noted lapse.  Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that Management comply with COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the accounting and reporting guidelines of the LDRRMF.  Further, we have recommended that Management submit the 2015 Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan and require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a special trust fund (GF) and Trust Liability – DRRM in the Trust Fund providing the necessary subsidiary ledgers and disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the Municipality the LDRRMF.

Finally, we have recommended Management to submit justification for the inconsistencies noted.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the basis in setting up the 2016 RPT/SET Receivable as well as the schedule to support the RPT/SET Receivable and the corresponding Deferred Income account balances as of December 31, 2016; record the proposed adjusting entry; and review and analyze the nature of the recorded transactions of the above-mentioned accounts and effect necessary adjustments, if any, to present fairly the financial statements.  The Municipal Treasurer should maintain/update Taxpayer’s Index Card to keep track of the yearly collection of real property tax.  The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer should also coordinate with one another to attain proper accounting of RPT as prescribed under NGAS.  The Municipal Treasurer should also coordinate and reconcile his records with that of the Municipal Assessor to determine any changes and/or updates in the real properties.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant sees to it that monthly accounts are submitted to the auditor’s office together with the paid vouchers/payrolls/official receipts in accordance with pertinent rules and regulations.  The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer be aware of all the requirements and accounts to be submitted to the Commission and the deadlines of submission of such accounts to avoid sanctions and delay of the audit. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor is advised to take a look into the possibility of assigning additional personnel in the Accounting Office to facilitate submission of accounts.

That the respective personnel in charge of the noted deficiencies submit the required supporting documents; and
Henceforth, that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer verify whether all necessary supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers before processing the claims, otherwise the claim should be returned to the respective employee/personnel for compliance of the requirements.

We have recommended that management revisit its policies/procedures in hiring job order workers and see to it that the same is aligned with the latest guidelines as contained under CSC-COA-DBM Joint memorandum Circular No. 1, s. 2017.  Meantime, submit the noted missing supporting documents for evaluation.

We have recommended that management furnish the Auditor copy of the purchase orders and contract documents not later than five (5) working days from issuance of purchase orders and perfection of contracts together with duly accomplished supporting documents for technical review and evaluation in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.
We have recommended that an Annual Procurement Plan be prepared to plan and schedule the procurement activities of the Municipality in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184.

We have recommended that the Municipality either retain an amount equivalent to at least ten percent (10%) of every progress payment or any of the authorized warranty security for the procurement of goods in order to ascertain that manufacturing defects, if it falls within the prescribed period of warranty, shall be borne and corrected by the supplier.

We have recommended that the agency officials justify why the procedural limits provided under R.A. No. 9184 were exceeded.  We also recommend that purchases beyond the threshold on shopping and small value procurements be done through public bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR.  Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in the highly exceptional cases provided for in R.A. No. 9184.  In all instances, the procuring entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price for the Government is obtained.

We have recommended that management prepare the LDRRMFIP annually for disaster risk reduction management program presenting the lump-sum 30% allocation for Quick Response Fund and allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded.

We have recommended that management create a special trust fund to be maintained solely for LDRRMF. The Municipal Accountant should record in a separate book, the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to properly track the utilization of the fund.
We have recommended that management submit the necessary GAD Plan and Budget and the accomplishment report related to the utilization for GAD for audit purposes.

observations.managementAction: Management started the conduct of physical count of property, plant and equipment and showed an initial report on the physical count.

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our previous recommendation and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant:

To religiously prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements supported with copies of debit/credit memos from the bank; 
To immediately submit the debit and credit memos pertinent to the BRS submitted to COA; and 
To request for authorization for the transfer of the amount of ₱11,820.00 from GF to TF depository account.
observations.managementAction: Some of the reconciling items were adjusted accordingly in 2016.
Management submitted some of the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management comply with DPWH DO No. 22, s. 2015 on the computation of VAT.  Also, we have recommended Management to submit justification on the non-compliance with DO No. 22, s. 2015.
observations.managementAction: The succeeding Approved Budget of the Contract and Program of Work was prepared using the revised percentage for VAT computation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Engineer justify the noted lapses henceforth; prepare Program of Work in accordance with the standard design set under the DPWH DO No. 11 s. 2014.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer claimed that the various concreting projects implemented were existing roads wherein providing roads shoulders could not be possible.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our previous recommendation and Management agreed to comply with the revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB Resolution 018-2006.  Further, we have recommended that Management submit justifications on the noted deficiencies and submit the lacking documents for validation purposes.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted some of the required supporting documents and promises to comply with the observations indicated regarding the implementation of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and deduct the 10% retention money from the gross amount for all succeeding payment of progress/final billing.  Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by RA No. 9184.

In view of the above noted observations, we have reiterated our previous recommendation as follows:

Justify the noted lapses and submit the required documents; 
The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer verify whether all necessary supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers before the processing of claims; and
All disbursements not attached with the required supporting documents should not be process and should be returned to their respective employee/personnel.
observations.managementAction: Some of the required supporting documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our previous recommendation that agency officials submit written explanation for splitting the procurement into smaller portions.

Further, we have recommended that the members of the Bid and Awards Committee should attend training/ updates on RA 9184 on the Government Procurement Law to familiarize themselves with the rules and regulations on government procurement.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that Management comply with Section 272 of RA No. 7160 on the use of SEF and implement identified program/project/activities as planned.  We have also recommended Management to submit justifications on the observation noted.
observations.managementAction: Management claimed that regravelling project was for pathways inside the school premise.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management submit justification/s with supporting documents on the observation noted.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that Management implement projects received from external sources promptly and ensure that funds are utilized for the intended purpose.  We have also recommended that Management submit justifications on the noted deficiencies and submit the list of beneficiaries together with duly signed acknowledgement receipt of the livelihood projects and farm equipment for validation purposes.
observations.managementAction: Management claimed that the proposed BUB projects were not implemented due to issues that arose among the beneficiary organizations. Management agreed to submit the Audit Team with copy of the letter informing the source agency why the fund intended for the proposed projects remained idle. Management also claimed that the distribution of chicks for livelihood projects as well as farm implements were witnessed by the officials from the Department of Agriculture and agreed to submit the Acknowledgment Receipts required.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management comply with Sections 1 and 6 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 10121 and expedite the creation and manning of the MDRRMO to ensure the achievement of the objectives of RA No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: Management claimed that they already passed the Local Ordinance creating the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Office.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our previous recommendation that the Management comply with DBM/DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 and submit justifications why the expenses should be considered under the 20% Development Fund.  Further, we have recommended that Management implement projects in accordance with said Circular in accordance with Annual Investment Plan in order to attain the purpose for which the fund was established.  Finally, we have recommended that Management require the Accountant to maintain a special trust fund in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund to facilitate monitoring of its utilization and ensure that the fund is used for authorized and eligible programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: Management claimed that reported accomplishment for Asphalt Overlay amounting to ₱2,358,607.15, which is not included in the Plan, was mistakenly reported as utilization and agreed to be more cautious in preparing the related utilization report.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that management:

Justify the above noted observations; 
Implement projects as programmed in the approved GAD Plan; and
Utilize GAD fund only for projects which will cater gender issues in the Municipality.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its purchases and contracts as mandated by laws, rules and regulations to avoid imposition of sanctions by the Bureau of Internal Revenue.
observations.managementAction: Management observed the payment modes in accordance with Revenue Regulations.
